title: BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 100 Junior Telecom Officer Posts
short_info: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL), a Government of India Enterprise, has released an official advertisement for 100 posts of Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] from the open market.

application_fee.details: Fee must be paid through online mode only (Internet Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card) on the BSNL website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in. Fee once paid is non-refundable.

age_limit.details: General Age Limit: Minimum 20 years and maximum 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill the minimum qualifications specified for the Telecom stream: | Core Qualification (Telecom Stream): Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent from a Central Govt./State Govt. recognized Institution/University in any of the following disciplines: Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, Information Technology (IT), or Instrumentation. Alternative Qualification: M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science). Higher Qualifications: Candidates possessing M.Tech. in the prescribed disciplines are also eligible to appear in the Direct Recruitment examination. Vision Requirement: The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision. Nationality: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

salary_details.pay scale: IDA Pay Scale [E-1] of ₹16,400 – ₹40,500/- (2nd PRC pay scale w.e.f. 01.01.2007)
salary_details.bond: ₹6 Lakhs bond to serve BSNL for a minimum of 5 years from date of appointment
salary_details.details: Essential administrative details regarding the vacancy package include: | *Reservation as per Central Govt. guidelines applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/EWS/Ex-Serviceman candidates.

how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must complete the online registration process as follows:
Visit the BSNL official examination website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in and register online only. No manual/paper applications will be entertained.

Keep the following ready before registering: valid mobile number and email ID (for OTP), scanned passport size colour photograph (jpg/jpeg, 20–50 kb, white background), scanned signature (jpg/jpeg, 10–20 kb), Class 10th Certificate (date of birth proof), Degree Certificate and consolidated marksheet, photo identity proof (Aadhaar/Passport/Driving License/Voter ID), valid category/reservation certificate as applicable, and Medical Certificate for PwBD candidates.
Pay the requisite non-refundable examination fee online through Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card.
After completing registration, take a printout of the computer-generated registration form and retain it for future reference.
An editing window will be open for 7 days after the registration period closes (July 4–11, 2026). Selective fields can be edited during this period only.

Candidates currently employed in Government Departments/Public Sector Undertakings/Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their present employer at the time of document verification.
Candidates must select up to 21 Circle preferences (in order of preference) from the registration portal dropdown menu. Options once exercised shall be final.
Admit cards can be downloaded from the BSNL website using the registered username and password well before the examination date.

faq.question: Q1. What is BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 is a direct recruitment drive for Junior Telecom Officer (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] posts in Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited from the open market. A total of 100 vacancies will be filled across various BSNL Circles under Advt. No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2026-RECTT-CO.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in BSNL JTO (Telecom) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 100 JTO (Telecom) vacancies distributed circle‑wise across BSNL’s territorial units. Major circles with higher intake include Karnataka (26 posts), Kerala (15 posts), Maharashtra (16 posts) and Madhya Pradesh (10 posts), while other circles have 1–6 posts each as per Table‑A.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration starts from 4 June 2026 at 10:00 AM and ends on 3 July 2026 at 10:00 AM. Candidates can edit their applications from 4 July 2026 (10:00 AM) to 11 July 2026 (10:00 AM), and the Computer Based Test (CBT) is tentatively scheduled in August 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility (qualification) for BSNL JTO (Telecom) 2026?
faq.answer: For the Telecom stream, candidates must have a B.E./B.Tech degree (or equivalent) in Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, IT or Instrumentation from a recognized institution. Alternatively, M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science) degree holders are also eligible, and those with M.Tech in the prescribed disciplines may also apply.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The basic age limit is 20 to 30 years as on the closing date of application. Relaxations apply as follows: OBC (NCL) up to 33 years, SC/ST up to 35 years, PwBD up to 40–45 years, and further relaxations for Ex‑Servicemen and BSNL employees as per Central Government and BSNL rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary and bond condition for BSNL JTO (Telecom) posts?
faq.answer: Selected JTO (Telecom) candidates will be appointed in the IDA Pay Scale E‑1: ₹16,400 – ₹40,500 (2nd PRC, w.e.f. 01.01.2007) with usual allowances as admissible. They will be on a two‑year probation and must execute a service bond of ₹6 lakhs to serve BSNL for a minimum period of 5 years from the date of appointment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the exam pattern for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT) of 3 hours, containing 120 objective questions for 480 marks. Section I: Engineering Stream–I – 50 questions, 200 marks Section II: Engineering Stream–II – 50 questions, 200 marks Section III: General Ability Test – 20 questions, 80 marks Each correct answer carries +4 marks, and each wrong answer attracts –1 mark (negative marking).

faq.question: Q8. What are the minimum qualifying marks in BSNL JTO CBT 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must secure at least 40% marks in each section individually. Overall, UR/OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates must obtain 50% aggregate, while SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates need at least 45% aggregate across all three sections to be considered for merit list preparation.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application fee for BSNL JTO (Telecom) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee is ₹2,000 for UR/OBC/EWS candidates and ₹1,000 for SC/ST/PwBD candidates. The fee is non‑refundable and must be paid online only via Internet Banking, Credit Card or Debit Card on www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible Indian nationals must apply online only at www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in during the registration window. Candidates should register with a valid mobile number and email ID, upload scanned photo and signature, fill academic and personal details, select up to 21 Circle preferences, pay the online fee, and finally print and retain the registered application for future reference. An editing window from 4–11 July 2026 allows limited corrections, but Circle preferences and some critical fields may not be editable.
